title: Flip a dictionary
prompt: Write invert_dict(d) that swaps keys and values: invert_dict({'a':'x','b':'y'}) is {'x':'a','y':'b'}. Assume values are unique.

help.plain: Build a new dict where each old value becomes a key and each old key becomes its value.
help.analogy: Turning a name->phone book into a phone->name reverse lookup.
help.mnemonic: For k,v in d.items(): put v->k.
help.nudge: How do you loop over both keys and values of a dict at once?
